steps.title: Identify the clause structure around the blank
steps.content: The blank connects a singular subject (“Museum of Modern Art”) to an object within a dependent clause introduced by “that.” The verb must be finite to serve as the clause's main predicate.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb requirement
steps.content: ”Houses” is the finite verb in third-person singular form, matching the singular subject. “Housing,” “to house,” and “to have housed” are non-finite forms—a participle and two infinitives—that cannot function as a clause's main verb. That matches choice D — D is correct.
